sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Renal and ureteric stones: assessment and management
sources.population: Adults, children, and young people with suspected or confirmed renal or ureteric stones, with age and pregnancy qualifiers.
sources.clinical_setting: Assessment, imaging, procedural management, metabolic testing, prevention, and follow-up of renal and ureteric stones.
sources.applicability_note: Exact only for selected stone identity, imaging, stone-analysis, metabolic-workup, hydration-context, and clinician-discussed prevention documentation. Acute symptom assessment, treatment, procedures, fixed follow-up, and UAE pathways remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_sections.heading: Diagnostic imaging
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-reviewed CT or ultrasound imaging with age and pregnancy qualifiers.

exact_sections.heading: Metabolic testing
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports stone analysis, serum-calcium context, and qualified paediatric specialist metabolic assessment.

exact_sections.heading: Preventing recurrence
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting hydration or dietary context and prevention advice only when discussed by the clinician; numeric instructions are not mapped.
